mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production indicates contraction in manufacturing and corporate profits, which depresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO 3-month change versus 20-day forward return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the proportion of negative returns following a decline is not statistically above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it signals that labor market weakening has become severe enough to reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns exceeds 50% or the average return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in housing starts (below 1000 thousand units) and rising unemployment (above 5%) indicates deep demand contraction that depresses corporate profits, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: housing bust and labor market deterioration
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the proportion of positive 20-day returns exceeds 50% or the median return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing regime defined by a 3-month decline in the federal funds rate, lower discount rates improved financial conditions and support equity prices, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing regime defined by 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS has declined over 3 months, the fraction of positive 5-day returns is not significantly above 50% (binomial test, p>0.05) or the average return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month rise in the CPI inflation index erodes real purchasing power and triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of CPIAUCSL 3-month change versus 20-day forward return is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the decile spread in returns between high and low inflation regimes is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 800 thousand units annually, it signals a deep and persistent housing recession that weighs on consumer wealth and construction employment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After a HOUST print below 800, the average 60-day forward return is not negative and statistically different from zero at the 5% level, or the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint sequential declines in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production over one month signal simultaneous labor and output contraction, consistent with recession onset, and predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month decline and INDPRO month-over-month decline
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO decline month-over-month, the fraction of positive 20-day returns exceeds 50% or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPI year-over-year above 3%), a rise in the federal funds rate over 3 months imposes even tighter monetary conditions that contract economic activity and equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: high inflation regime with monetary tightening
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months and CPI Y/Y exceeds 3%, the average 5-day forward return is not negative or the proportion of positive returns is above 50%.
